evals.name: order-grid-column
evals.prompt: I've got a base Commerce app scaffolded with the aio-commerce-sdk (app.commerce.config.ts exists and the project is initialized). I need to add a 'Fulfillment' column to the Admin order grid — the value for each order comes from our warehouse API. Wire up the config and the runtime action handler.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.gridColumns declared in app.commerce.config.ts with a single column (id, label, type, align) and a runtimeAction in <package>/<action> form. A handler under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ using parseGridRequest + okGridResponse/errorGridResponse from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/grid-columns, with row keys matching the column id. The action registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml. Mentions running npx @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-app init and aio app build.
assertions: Declares adminUi.order.gridColumns in app.commerce.config.ts
Grid column has all required fields: id, label, type (one of boolean/date/datetime/float/integer/string), and align (one of left/center/right)
gridColumns includes a runtimeAction in <package>/<action> form
Handler imports parseGridRequest and okGridResponse (and/or errorGridResponse) from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/grid-columns
okGridResponse rows are keyed by entity id, and each row's value keys match the declared column id
Handler action source lives under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ (NOT src/commerce-extensibility-1/)
Action is registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml, with a function path relative to that folder
Notes mention running npx @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-app init and aio app build

evals.name: customer-mass-action-worker
evals.prompt: Add a bulk 'Archive' action to the customer grid in my Commerce admin app. When an admin selects some customers and clicks it, it should call a backend runtime action that archives them. The app is already initialized with the aio-commerce-sdk.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.customer.massActions with a type:"worker" entry (id, label, runtimeAction, optionally selectionLimit/confirm/notifications). A handler under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ using parseMassActionRequest + okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/mass-actions. Action registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml. Does NOT invent a view/path for a worker action.
assertions: Declares adminUi.customer.massActions as an array containing a type:"worker" entry
Worker mass action has required id, label, and runtimeAction (<package>/<action>) fields
Worker entry does NOT include view-only fields (no path, no sandboxPermissions)
Handler imports parseMassActionRequest and okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/mass-actions
Handler action source lives under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ and is registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml
Uses the v2 adminUi shape (no commerce/backend-ui/1 or adminUiSdk.registration legacy API)

evals.name: menu-and-order-view-button
evals.prompt: Two things for my Commerce admin app (already initialized with the SDK): add a custom dashboard page under the Sales menu, and add a 'Sync inventory' button on the order detail page that triggers a backend action to push the order to our warehouse.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.menu with id (valid charset), label, description, parentMenu: MENU_SALES imported from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/menu, and no handler (iframe into web-src). adminUi.order.viewButtons with a type:"worker" entry (id, label, runtimeAction) and a handler under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ using parseOrderViewButtonRequest + okOrderViewButtonResponse/orderViewButtonErrorResponse from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/order-view-buttons. Worker action registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml.
assertions: Declares adminUi.menu (single object) with id, label, and description
Sets parentMenu to MENU_SALES imported from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/menu
Menu id uses only valid characters (letters, digits, /, :, _) — no hyphens or spaces
Recognizes the menu has no runtime action handler (iframe into web-src); does not scaffold a handler action for the menu
Declares adminUi.order.viewButtons with a type:"worker" entry (id, label, runtimeAction)
View button handler imports parseOrderViewButtonRequest and okOrderViewButtonResponse/orderViewButtonErrorResponse from @adobe/aio-commerce-lib-admin-ui/order-view-buttons
Worker view-button action lives under src/commerce-backend-ui-2/actions/ and is registered under runtimeManifest in src/commerce-backend-ui-2/ext.config.yaml
